CapGainsLossTxInvstIncmGrp.PropertyDesc: .450 SHS OF CONS STAPLES SEL SECT SPDR FD

SumOfProgramRelatedInvstGrp.Description1Txt: IN 2021, THE ORGANIZATION PURCHASED TWO PARCELS OF LAND IN THE CITY HEIGHTS AREA REFERRED TO AS THE "THRIFT STORE PROPERTIES." THE FIRST, HUMBLE HEART, WAS PURCHASED ON FEBRUARY 25, 2021. THE SECOND, UNITED CEREBRAL PALSY OF SAN DIEGO, WAS PURCHASED ON JUNE 1, 2021. IN 2024, THE ORGANIZATION CONTRIBUTED THIS LAND AND BUILDINGS TO WAKELAND HOUSING & DEVELOPMENT CORP. TO BE USED FOR DEVELOPING AFFORDABLE HOUSING WITH A FAIR MARKET VALUE OF $4,225,377. IN CONNECTION WITH THIS CONTRIBUTION, THE ORGANIZATION ENTERED INTO AN AGREEMENT TO PROVIDE A PRI LOAN IN AN AMOUNT NOT TO EXCEED $18,000,000 WITH BELOW-MARKET INTEREST RATES TO WAKELAND HOUSING & DEVELOPMENT CORP. FOR THE DEVELOPMENT OF THE AFFORDABLE HOUSING. THE PRI LOAN WAS FUNDED IN THE AMOUNT OF $7,125,000 IN AUGUST 2024.
SumOfProgramRelatedInvstGrp.Description2Txt: THE ORGANIZATION ENTERED INTO TWO AGREEMENTS IN 2021 TO PROVIDE PRI LOANS WITH BELOW-MARKET INTEREST RATES TO WAKELAND HOUSING & DEVELOPMENT CORP. ONE OF THE PRI LOANS TO BE USED TO DEVELOP THE CONTRIBUTED REAL ESTATE FOR THE CONTRIBUTION WAS MADE ON DECEMBER 6, 2021 NOT TO EXCEED $14,250,000. THE PRI WAS FUNDED IN THE AMOUNT OF $7,125,000 ON DECEMBER 6, 2021. OF THE REMAINING AMOUNT, $7,125,000 WAS FUNDED IN 2024, COMPLETING THE OBLIGATION FOR THIS PRI. A RELATED REPAYMENT OF $155,037 WAS RECEIVED IN 2024.

ApplicationSubmissionInfoGrp.FormAndInfoAndMaterialsTxt: WE ASK POTENTIAL APPLICANTS TO SUBMIT A GRANT INQUIRY VIA PRICE PHILANTHROPIES FOUNDATION GRANT STAFF. AFTER AN INTERNAL SCREENING PROCESS, SOME APPLICANTS WILL BE INVITED TO SUBMIT A FORMAL GRANT APPLICATION. THE FORMAL GRANT REQUEST INCLUDES THE FOLLOWING INFORMATION: ORGANIZATION NAME, ADDRESS, PHONE, CONTACT INFORMATION; PROGRAM/PROJECT TITLE; PROGRAM/PROJECT DESCRIPTION; REQUEST AMOUNT; TOTAL PROGRAM/PROJECT BUDGET; SECURED/PENDING FUNDERS; AGE GROUP; GEOGRAPHICAL AREA; PROGRAM AREA; TYPE OF SUPPORT; KEY ISSUES; EXECUTIVE SUMMARY; TARGET AUDIENCE; PROGRAM DELIVERABLES; ANTICIPATED OUTCOMES; ATTACHMENTS TO INCLUDE: 501(C)(3) TAX EXEMPT LETTER; DETAILED PROGRAM/PROJECT BUDGET; ORGANIZATION FINANCIALS; ORGANIZATIONAL BUDGET FOR CURRENT FISCAL YEAR THE INITIAL GRANT REQUEST MUST BE SUBMITTED THROUGH OUR ONLINE SYSTEM.
ApplicationSubmissionInfoGrp.RestrictionsOnAwardsTxt: GRANTS MUST BE MADE TO NON-PROFIT ORGANIZATIONS WITH 501(C)(3) STATUS OR GOVERNMENTAL UNITS SUCH AS PUBLIC SCHOOLS OR CITY DEPARTMENTS OR FOR INHERENTLY CHARITABLE ACTIVITIES; GRANTS ARE NOT MADE (I) TO ORGANIZATIONS WHOSE PRIMARY PURPOSE IS RELIGIOUS, OR (II) FOR PROPAGANDIZING, INFLUENCING LEGISLATION AND/OR ELECTIONS, PROMOTING VOTING REGISTRATION, FOR POLITICAL CANDIDATES, POLITICAL CAMPAIGNS OR ORGANIZATIONS ENGAGED IN POLITICAL ACTIVITIES; OR (III) TO FEDERAL APPEALS OR TO ORGANIZATIONS THAT COLLECT FUNDS FOR REDISTRIBUTION TO OTHER NON-PROFIT GROUPS.

AllOtherProgramRelatedInvstGrp.Desc: AS NOTED IN STATEMENT 18, ANOTHER PRI LOAN AGREEMENT WITH WAKELAND WAS ENTERED INTO 2021. THE SECOND PRI LOAN WAS NOT TO EXCEED $8,085,000. IN 2021, $4,042,250 WAS FUNDED AND $2,830,000 WAS FUNDED IN 2023. IN 2024, THE REMAINING $1,212,750 WAS FUNDED AND $146,150 OF THE AMOUNT WAS PAID BACK.

ExpenditureResponsibilityGrp.PurposeOfGrantTxt: THE CAPITAL ENDOWMENT GRANT MUST BE EXPENDED FOR CHARITABLE, SCIENTIFIC, LITERARY, OR EDUCATIONAL PURPOSES AS DEFINED UNDER INTERNAL REVENUE CODE 501(C)(3), AND MORE SPECIFICALLY LIMITED TO SUPPORT AND OPERATE THE AARON PRICE FELLOWS PROGRAM. THE PROGRAM PREPARES HIGHLY MOTIVATED AND DIVERSE SAN DIEGO, CALIFORNIA, PUBLIC HIGH SCHOOL STUDENTS TO BE RESPONSIBLE, ENGAGED, AND CARING MEMBERS OF THEIR COMMUNITY BY FOSTERING FRIENDSHIPS BETWEEN FELLOWS FROM DIVERSE ETHNIC, RELIGIOUS, AND ECONOMIC BACKGROUNDS; TEACHING FELLOWS ABOUT GOVERNMENT, CULTURAL, BUSINESS, AND NON-PROFIT INSTITUTIONS; AND EXPOSING FELLOWS TO EXCITING CAREER OPPORTUNITIES. ANY FUNDS NOT SO EXPENDED OR MAINTAINED AS AN ENDOWMENT FOR THE BENEFIT OF THE AARON PRICE FELLOWS PROGRAM MUST BE RETURNED TO PRICE PHILANTHROPIES FOUNDATION. AS OF DECEMBER 31, 2024, $7,695,326 OF THE ORIGINAL GRANT OF $25,000,000 HAS BEEN EXPENDED, AND $1,382,900 OF THIS AMOUNT IS ATTRIBUTABLE TO THE CURRENT REPORTING PERIOD.
ExpenditureResponsibilityGrp.ResultsOfVerificationTxt: THE BOARD OF DIRECTORS OF THE PRICE PHILANTHROPIES FOUNDATION RECEIVED A REPORT FROM THE BOARD OF DIRECTORS OF THE AARON PRICE FELLOWS FOUNDATION DATED JANUARY 17, 2025 AS VERIFICATION OF THE ACTIVITIES OF THE AARON PRICE FELLOWS FOUNDATION.

ExpenditureResponsibilityGrp.PurposeOfGrantTxt: THE CAPITAL GRANT MUST BE EXPENDED FOR CHARITABLE, SCIENTIFIC, LITERARY, OR EDUCATIONAL PURPOSES AS DEFINED UNDER INTERNAL REVENUE CODE 501(C)(3), AND MORE SPECIFICALLY LIMITED TO SUPPORT AND OPERATE THE PRICESMART FOUNDATION. THE PROGRAM INVESTS IN CREATING AN ENVIRONMENT OF OPPORTUNITY AND ACCESS FOR COMMUNITIES IN REGIONS WHERE PRICESMART, INC. OPERATES, WITH A FOCUS ON PROVIDING ECONOMIC OPPORTUNITY, ENVIRONMENTAL AND COMMUNITY RESILIENCE, AND YOUTH DEVELOPMENT IN UNDER SERVED COMMUNITIES. GRANTEE AGREES TO EXPEND THE ENTIRE GRANT AWARD SOLEY FOR THE PURPOSE OF GRANTEE'S GENERAL OPERATIONS AND GRANTMAKING IN LATIN AMERICA AND THE CARIBBEAN. IN 2024, $1,800,000 WAS ISSUED FOR PROGRAM AND PROJECT SUPPORT AND AN ADDITIONAL $8,000 WAS ISSUED FOR GENERAL OPERATING SUPPORT FOR A TOTAL GRANT OF $1,808,000. OF THIS AMOUNT, $125,000 WAS REFUNDED TO PRICE PHILANTHROPIES FOUNDATION. AS OF DECEMBER 31, 2024, THE FULL AMOUNT OF THE GRANT WAS EXPENDED.
ExpenditureResponsibilityGrp.ResultsOfVerificationTxt: THE BOARD OF DIRECTORS OF THE PRICE PHILANTHROPIES FOUNDATION RECEIVED A REPORT FROM THE BOARD OF DIRECTORS OF THE PRICESMART FOUNDATION DATED JANUARY 30, 2025 AS VERIFICATION OF THE ACTIVITIES OF THE AARON PRICE FELLOWS FOUNDATION.

GainLossSaleOtherAssetGrp.AssetDesc: UVR - FF&E - UNIT 209 CARPET REPLACEMENT (FIRST FLOOR ONLY)

GeneralExplanationGrp.MediumExplanationTxt: 1(A)(4): DURING THE REPORTING PERIOD, THE ORGANIZATION PAID MANAGEMENT FEES TO THE PRICE GROUP, LLC. CERTAIN MEMBERS OF THE PRICE GROUP, LLC ARE ALSO DIRECTORS AND OFFICERS OF THE ORGANIZATION. NO COMPENSATION IS PAID DIRECTLY FROM THE ORGANIZATION TO THE MEMBERS OF THE PRICE GROUP, LLC.INSTEAD, CERTAIN MEMBERS ARE COMPENSATED FROM THE LLC. THE MANAGEMENT SERVICES RENDERED TO THE ORGANIZATION WERE MADE ON A BASIS NO MORE FAVORABLE THAN THAT ON WHICH SUCH SERVICES ARE PROVIDED IN THE MARKET PLACE.SEE IRC SECTION 4941(D)(2)(D).

THE CITY HEIGHTS REAL ESTATE PROGRAM PROVIDES A SAFE, CLEAN, AND AFFORDABLE LIVING AND WORK ENVIRONMENT THROUGH THE ACQUISITION AND CONSTRUCTION OF SEVERAL REAL ESTATE PROJECTS IN THE CITY HEIGHTS AREA OF THE CITY OF SAN DIEGO, CA. SUCH PROJECTS INCLUDE THE CONSTRUCTION AND MANAGEMENT OF: A 116 UNIT APARTMENT COMPLEX; A SIX-STORY (119,000 SQ FOOT) OFFICE BUILDING; A MIXED-USE RESIDENTIAL (92 UNITS) AND RETAIL (20,000 SQ FOOT) PROJECT; AND VARIOUS PARCELS OF LAND HELD FOR FUTURE PROJECTS. THE APRENDER Y CRECER PROGRAM PROVIDES SCHOOL SUPPLIES TO STUDENTS AND TEACHERS AND CLEANING SUPPLIES TO SCHOOLS IN VARIOUS CITIES IN CENTRAL AMERICA.THE CITY HEIGHTS INITIATIVE IS A PARTNERSHIP BETWEEN PRICE PHILANTHROPIES FOUNDATION AND PUBLIC AND NON-PROFIT AGENCIES IN THE CITY HEIGHTS AREA OF SAN DIEGO, CALIFORNIA TO PROVIDE OPPORTUNITIES FOR CITY HEIGHTS YOUTH AND FAMILIES TO PROSPER. THE INITIATIVE TAKES A HOLISTIC APPROACH TO IMPROVING RESIDENTS OF CITY HEIGHTS QUALITY OF LIFE THROUGH ACCESS TO HEALTH AND SOCIAL SERVICES, QUALITY EDUCATION, JOBS, LEGAL REPRESENTATION AND SAFE NEIGHBORHOODS.CARDINALS INTERACT IS A MENTORING AND TUTORING PROGRAM RUN BY PRICE PHILANTHROPIES FOUNDATION FOR STUDENTS AT HOOVER HIGH SCHOOL IN SAN DIEGO, CALIFORNIA. ITS MISSION IS TO HELP STUDENTS REACH THEIR MAXIMUM POTENTIAL ACADEMICALLY AND PERSONALLY AND ASSIST THEM IN DEVELOPING SKILLS IN THE AREAS OF TEAMWORK, SELF-ESTEEM, DECISION MAKING AND COMMUNICATION.THE EXPENSES LISTED IN THE SUMMARY OF DIRECT CHARITABLE ACTIVITIES (PART VIII-A) INCLUDE ALL EXPENSES RELATED TO THE PROGRAMS LISTED, INCLUDING SALARIES AND BENEFITS.

ON JULY 31, 2024, THE ORGANIZATION SET UP A DONOR ADVISED FUND AT THE JEWISH COMMUNITY FOUNDATION. THE ORGANIZATION FUNDED THE DONOR ADVISED FUND WITH THE CONTRIBUTION OF PRICESMART, INC. STOCK. THE VALUE OF THE CONTRIBUTION WAS $19,142,550 AND IS REFLECTED WITHIN GRANT EXPENSE FOR THE YEAR ENDED DECEMBER 31, 2024. NONE OF THE OFFICERS AND DIRECTORS OF PRICE PHILANTHROPIES FOUNDATION ARE AN OFFICER OR DIRECTOR OF THE JEWISH COMMUNITY FOUNDATION.

AS NOTED IN STATEMENT 18, THE ORGANIZATION ENTERED INTO TWO AGREEMENTS IN 2021 TO PROVIDE PRI LOANS TO WAKELAND HOUSING & DEVELOPMENT CORP. ANOTHER ONE OF THE PRI LOANS FOR THE REAL ESTATE CONTRIBUTION MADE ON AUGUST 31, 2021 WAS NOT TO EXCEED $8,085,000. IN 2021, 4,042,250 WAS FUNDED AND AN ADDITIONAL $2,830,000 WAS FUNDED IN 2023. IN 2024, $1,212,750 WAS FUNDED AND $146,150 OF THE AMOUNT WAS PAID BACK.
